violations.text: Shellstock (little necks) located in (sauté prep unit) did not have the proper identification tags. Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the shellstock are received until the container is empty. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2024

Shellstock tags did not have all of the required information. The following was observed: 1) The last date of sale was lacking on the shellstock tags. 2) The tags were not in chronological order from the last date of sale. Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2024

Tomatoes were observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit in the grill prep unit. The grill prep unit was observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 43-51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, tomatoes were kept in the unit overnight. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2024

The grill prep unit located in the cookline area was not in good repair. The grill prep unit was observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 43-51 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit taken out of service until it can be repaired/replaced/serviced. At the time of inspection, PIC contacted maintenance for repairs. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2024

The following was observed of non-food contact surfaces: 1) The side of the oven observed with an accumulation of food debris 2) The ice machine was observed with an accumulation of biofilm Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned as often as necessary. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2024
